sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['N', 'c', 'W', 'G', 'b', 'g', 'P']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, bbox=({min(rows)},{min(cols)})-({max(rows)},{max(cols)}), children={n['children']}")

# Also check adjacency
print("\nAdjacency:")
for pair in seg['adjacency_list']:
    i, j = pair
    ci = cj = None
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['id'] == i: ci = n['color']
        if n['id'] == j: cj = n['color']
    if ci and ci not in ['P', 'c'] and cj and cj not in ['P', 'c']:  # skip HUD
        print(f"  {i}({ci}) - {j}({cj})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

The game has 3 main objects:
1. Left square: blue frame with gray center (28x28 to 31x39)
2. Merged object: charcoal frame with 2 blue centers (24x44 to 31x47)
3. Carried square: white frame with blue center (moves with player)
The player can move around and push objects with the white strip. The SPACE key attaches/detaches the carried square.
Maybe the goal is to arrange the 3 squares in a specific pattern. Or maybe the goal is to merge all 3 squares into one object.
Let me try a completely different approach: what if I need to push the left square INTO the merged object? The left square is at (28,28)-(31,39), and the merged object is at (24,44)-(31,47). They're not adjacent, but if I push the left square to the right, it would eventually reach the merged object.

Then I can move RIGHT to push the left square towards the merged object.
Actually wait, let me reconsider. The white strip is at (15,48)-(19,51), which is rows 15-19. The left square is at (28,28)-(31,39), which is rows 28-31. The white strip is above the left square, not to the left of it.
To push the left square to the right, I need the white strip to be to the left of the left square, at the same row range. So I need to move DOWN to get the white strip to rows 28-31, and then move LEFT to get it to cols < 28.
Let me try: move DOWN to get the white strip to the same row as the left square, then move LEFT to position it to the left of the left square, then move RIGHT to push the left square towards the merged object.
Actually, I realize I've been wasting too many actions. Let me try a more direct approach: move the player to the left of the left square and push it right.
The left square is at (28,28)-(31,39). To push it right, I need the white strip to be at rows 28-31 and cols < 28.
Currently, the player is at (12,48)-(14,51) and the white strip is at (15,48)-(19,51).
To get the white strip to rows 28-31, I need to move DOWN. The white strip is at rows 15-19, so I need to move DOWN about 9 rows to get it to rows 24-28. But the player is at rows 12-14, so moving DOWN 9 times would put the player at rows 21-23 and the white strip at rows 24-28.
Wait, but the white strip is below the player (because the last movement was RIGHT, and the white strip is in the opposite direction... no wait, the white strip is below the player because the last movement was UP, and the white strip is in the opposite direction).
Actually, I'm confused about the white strip's position relative to the player. Let me just try moving DOWN and see what happens.
